quality.rationale: 基于热力学第一定律与定熵导数关系的多步推导，逻辑链条完整且适用于宇宙学膨胀气体模型

origin.question: Problem 5.33. For a perfect Maxwell-Boltzmann gas, show that if $\gamma$ is approximately constant in some regime of interest, then $p = Kn^{\gamma}$ and $\rho = mn + Kn^{\gamma} / (\gamma - 1)$ under adiabatic conditions. (K = constant, m = mass of particles.)
origin.solution: Solution 5.33. From Problem 5.32,$$\gamma = \Gamma_{1} = \left. \frac {\partial \log p}{\partial \log n}\right|_{s}$$so if $\gamma =$ constant,$$\mathbf {p} = \mathbf {K n}^{\gamma}.$$For adiabatic changes the first law of thermodynamics is$$\mathrm {d} \rho = \frac {\rho + \mathrm {p}}{\mathrm {n}} \mathrm {d n} = \left(\frac {\rho}{\mathrm {n}} + \mathrm {K n}^{\gamma - 1}\right) \mathrm {d n},$$or$$\frac {\mathrm {d}}{\mathrm {d} n} \left(\frac {\rho}{n}\right) = K n^{\gamma - 2}$$so that the solution is$$\frac {\rho}{n} = \frac {K n^{\gamma - 1}}{\gamma - 1} + c o n s t a n t.$$But when $\mathfrak{n} \to 0$ , then $\rho / \mathfrak{n} \to \mathfrak{m}$ , so$$\rho = \operatorname {m n} + \frac {\operatorname {K n}^{\gamma}}{\gamma - 1}.$$
